mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least one month, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced refinancing capacity. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.59% indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.7% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously decline, indicating weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint deterioration predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings guidance contracts.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1800 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 361046M and HOUST at 2120K units
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumption and investment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at a rate exceeding 1% month-over-month, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate margin expansion and reduced input cost pressures.
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 48% of periods with INDPRO MoM growth > 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, indicating labor market slack emergence. This interaction signals deteriorating economic momentum and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100K AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 134966K and UNRATE at 4.7%
regime_hint: labor market deceleration phase preceding broader economic slowdown
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (PAYEMS × UNRATE) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, creating a real rate tightening regime. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in eventual rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth declines > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 199.1 and FEDFUNDS at 4.59%
regime_hint: disinflation phase with elevated nominal rates creating real rate compression opportunity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle weakness. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as consensus earnings estimates are revised downward in response to slowing aggregate demand.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances with GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, indicating stagflationary pressure. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums upward due to margin compression and demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 111.2088 and CPIAUCSL at 199.1
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with simultaneous supply contraction and persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-triggered instances, or the stagflation interaction term (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.
